claim: When agents choose tools in production, documentation quality becomes a primary differentiator for dev tools adoption, not developer word-of-mouth
actionable: Optimize your dev tool's documentation for AI agent parsing: use structured examples, clear use-case headers, and API specifications that maximize LLM retrievability
source_title: The AI Agent Economy Is Here

claim: Small thinker LLM + large model pattern: use a small model for conversation flow decisions, issue one tool call to a larger model for complex requests; keeps latency budget tight while preserving capability
actionable: Implement a two-tier LLM routing: deploy a small model (7-13B) for turn-taking and intent detection, route complex tasks to a 70B+ model via single API calls to minimize round-trips
source_title: The AI Agent Economy Is Here

claim: Agents have already become economic actors making purchasing decisions for dev tools; the market shift from 20M human developers to billions of humans + their agents is underway
actionable: Build your SaaS with agent affordances first: prioritize API-driven workflows, avoid JavaScript-heavy UIs, design tool discovery for programmatic evaluation by LLMs
source_title: The AI Agent Economy Is Here

claim: Agents struggle to hold relationships and people avoid chatting with agents despite hallucinating; chat alone is not a viable interaction model for agent engagement
actionable: Design agent-human touchpoints around structured tasks (e.g., approval workflows, data retrieval) rather than open-ended chat; minimize conversation requirements
source_title: The AI Agent Economy Is Here
